content.full_text:
t

RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

July 18, 1996

Todd A. Goluba
Atkinson, Andelson, Loya, Ruud & Romo
The Atrium, Suite 200
5776 Stoneridge Mall Road
Pleasanton, California 94588

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-208

Dear Mr. Goluba:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Manteca Unified School District Boardmember William Goodwin
regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

In addition to his position on the Manteca Unified School
District Board of Trustees, Mr. Goodwin is employed by the San
Joaquin County Office of Education ("SJCOE"). May Mr. Goodwin
participate in Board of Trustees decisions which may result in
additional students being referred to programs administered by the
SJCOE?

CONCLUSION

Mr. Goodwin is not disqualified from participating in
decisions that may materially affect the SJCOE because his salary
from the SJCOE is government income and therefore exempt from the
conflict of interest provisions of the Act. This advice is
limited to the provisions of the Political Reform Act.

FACTS

In addition to his services as an elected official with
Manteca Unified School District ("MUSD"), Mr. Goodwin is employed

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations
appear at Title 2, Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.

File No. A-96-208
Page 2

by the San Joaquin County Office of Education ("SJCOE") as a "Job
Developer," a paid, hourly position in which he helps place
students in jobs in the community. In his capacity as a MUSD
Boardmember, Mr. Goodwin may from time to time participate in
board decisions to expel pupils from MUSD. When the board expels
a student, it also frequently refers that student to an
alternative educational placement with the "county court and
community schools" programs operated by the SJCOE. Mr. Goodwin,
in his capacity as a "Job Developer" with SJCOE, assists many of
these same students by helping them locate jobs in the community.
Mr. Goodwin earns in excess of $250 per year as an SJCOE employee.

SJCOE receives a financial benefit from increased student
enrollment in its county court and community schools programs in
the form of a greater funding apportionment from the State of
California. Such apportionments are tied to the average daily
attendance of students in the programs. Thus, the more students
who are enrolled and in attendance in SJCOE programs, the more the
SJCOE receives in funding, and vice versa.

In our telephone conversation on June 28, 1996, you explained
that the SJCOE is formed pursuant to the California Education Code
as part of the state and county system of education.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. Section 87103 specifies that an official has
a financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from effect on the public generally, on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public of-
ficial has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institu-
tion in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made.

File No. A-96-208
Page 3


     (d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

     (e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by a public official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made. The amount of the value of
gifts specified by this subdivision shall be
adjusted biennially by the commission to equal the
same amount determined by the commission pursuant
to subdivision (f) of Section 89503.

                         (Section 87103.)

     In your letter, you stated that Mr. Goodwin is a member of
the MUSD and a public official subject to the conflict of interest
provisions of the Act. In addition, Mr. Goodwin is employed in an
hourly position as Job Developer for the SJCOE. The SJCOE is a
public agency formed and operated under the provisions of the
California Education Code. Thus, the SJCOE would be considered a
local government agency under the Act. (Section 82041.)2

     Salary reimbursement for expenses or per diem received
from a state, local, or federal government agency is expressly
exempted from the definition of "income" for purposes of the Act.
(Section 82030(b)(2).) Thus, the salary Mr. Goodwin receives from
the SJCOE would not create a conflict of interest with respect to
decisions affecting the SJCOE. (Section 87103(c).)

     Absent some other disqualifying financial interest as set
forth in Section 87103, above, or some direct financial effect on
Mr. Goodwin personally resulting from a governmental decision,
Mr. Goodwin may participate in the Board of Trustee's decisions
concerning students who may be referred to programs administered
by SJCOE.




_________________________
2  A local government agency is defined in Section 82041 as "a
county, city or district of any kind including school district, or
any other local or regional political subdivision, or any
department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other
agency of the foregoing."

File No. A-96-208
Page 4


     If you have any further questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Liane Randolph
                                             Counsel, Legal Division
